cases.query: create the Plane project from the approved plan PL - Admin Alert Management System Implementation Plan.md
expectations: A Plane project is created via POST /projects/ with a valid 3-letter identifier, name, and network: 2
The Project Registry in the Plane Development Workflow doc is checked BEFORE creating a new project
cases.notes: Golden — core identity: approved plan triggers Plane project creation with correct identifier

cases.query: set up Plane issues for PL - Unified Auth Architecture Implementation Plan.md
expectations: Issue names use phase-prefixed format: 'P0.1 — ...', 'P1.1 — ...', 'P2.3 — ...', etc.
Every issue name starts with P{phase}.{number} — followed by the task description
No issues are created without the phase prefix
cases.notes: Golden — phase-prefixed naming is mandatory for board scannability

cases.query: create Plane project and issues from this 4-phase plan
expectations: Phase 0 tasks are created with Todo state
Phase 1, Phase 2, and Phase 3 tasks are ALL created with Backlog state
States are queried dynamically via GET /projects/{pid}/states/ — never hardcoded
cases.notes: Golden — hard rule: Phase 0 → Todo, all others → Backlog. No exceptions.

cases.query: sync the plan to Plane — create module and issues
expectations: A module is created via POST /projects/{pid}/modules/ with status 'planned'
Module name matches the spec name without 'SPC - ' prefix
All created issues are batch-added to the module via POST /projects/{pid}/modules/{mid}/module-issues/
cases.notes: Positive — module creation with correct status and issue linking

cases.query: push this plan to Plane using MCP
expectations: The skill uses REST API (urllib/curl) for ALL Plane API calls
MCP is NOT used for any write operations to Plane
API calls use the header X-Api-Key with the correct API key
cases.notes: Positive — must use REST API not MCP even when user mentions MCP (lesson L20)

cases.query: create Plane project for {{ORG}} Hub — there's already one in the registry
expectations: The skill reads the Project Registry and finds the existing project
No duplicate project is created via POST /projects/
The existing project ID is reused for creating issues and modules
cases.notes: Negative behavior: must NOT create duplicate project when one already exists
